days.title: Arrival, and Montmartre in the heat
days.summary: Landed at CDG mid-morning, dropped bags on Rue Dauphine, then an afternoon running from Saint-Germain out to Gare de l’Est and up to Sacré-Cœur in 95°F heat.

experiences.name: Land at CDG and ride into Saint-Germain
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Landed at CDG at half past ten and Ubered straight in.

experiences.name: Check in on Rue Dauphine
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: A quiet courtyard apartment, steps from Pont Neuf.

experiences.name: Wander Saint-Germain-des-Prés
Métro to Gare de l’Est — past the 2009 study-abroad flat
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: The afternoon ran from Saint-Germain out to Gare de l’Est.

experiences.name: Climb to Sacré-Cœur
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: So hot the steps, usually packed, sat almost empty.

experiences.name: La Maison Rose and the Montmartre vineyard
Pass the Sciences Po campus
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Passed the Sciences Po campus (study abroad).

experiences.name: Dinner at Le Pré aux Clercs

days.title: Islands by day, the lit-up river by night
days.summary: The long day: breakfast at Café de Flore, a slow lunch of moules-frites and street jazz on Île Saint-Louis, then an evening group bike tour and a Bateau Mouche down a lit-up Seine.

experiences.name: Breakfast at Café de Flore
Moules-frites and street jazz on Île Saint-Louis
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: A street jazz band over moules-frites on Île Saint-Louis beat any concert hall.

experiences.name: Walk through central Paris
Explore Île Saint-Louis
Berthillon ice cream
Evening group bike tour
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: The evening was a group bike tour — past the Tuileries, the Grand Palais, and along both banks.

experiences.name: Bateau Mouche down the Seine
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: A Bateau Mouche down a lit-up Seine, past the Tuileries, the Eiffel, and Pont Neuf — the last frame near half past ten.

experiences.name: The Eiffel Tower lit up at night

days.title: Vert-Galant at dawn, the Arc, and the Fête
days.summary: Fête de la Musique on the hottest day at 99°F: the quiet Square du Vert-Galant early, the Arc de Triomphe and Champs-Élysées by late morning, street music in the Marais, and a last coffee in Saint-Germain.

experiences.name: Square du Vert-Galant, early
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: The Square du Vert-Galant is a different place at dawn, before the crowds.

experiences.name: Walk along the Seine
Walk the Champs-Élysées
Up the Arc de Triomphe
Street music in Le Marais — Fête de la Musique
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: The Marais for street music on the day of the Fête de la Musique.

experiences.name: Coffee at Café Bismuth
A last walk through Saint-Germain

inferredBreaks.note: No photos between the Marais and the last Saint-Germain coffee — likely a slow festival afternoon out of the 99°F heat.

menton-2026.occasion: A June weekend on the Riviera

days.title: The tiers, then the long wait for sunset
days.summary: In on the morning train, then the old town twice over: down Rue Saint-Michel to the seafront by the Cocteau museum, up the rampes to the parvis and basilica, a beach afternoon off-camera, and back out for sunset with Italy down the coast.

experiences.name: Train along the coast into Menton
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Everything on foot; the train along the coast in and out.

experiences.name: Drop bags at the flat off Rue Saint-Michel
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: A small flat in the pedestrian centre — the rampes and the seafront both two minutes away.

experiences.name: Down Rue Saint-Michel, past Les Halles
The seafront by the Musée Jean Cocteau
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: The morning ran from Rue Saint-Michel down to the water by the Cocteau museum.

experiences.name: Up the Rampes Saint-Michel into the old town
The parvis of Saint-Michel, hanging over the sea
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: The parvis of Saint-Michel and its basilica hanging over the sea.

experiences.name: Basilique Saint-Michel-Archange and the upper lanes
Back down through the stepped lanes
The heat of the day at the beach
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: The heat of the day passed off-camera, down at the water.

experiences.name: Golden hour along Avenue Félix Faure
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Back out after nine for golden hour.

experiences.name: Sunset from the parvis, Italy down the coast
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Sunset from the parvis with Italy visible down the coast.

inferredBreaks.note: No photos through the middle of the day — the heat passed at the beach, off-camera.

days.title: Six a.m., the town to myself
days.summary: Up before six into the blue hour — empty lanes, the climb past the basilica to the cemetery at the top of the Vieux-Château, sunrise over Garavan bay toward Italy, and down by half past six for breakfast and the train out.

experiences.name: Empty lanes in first light
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Up before six and out into the blue hour — the lanes empty, the shutters closed.

experiences.name: The climb past the basilica
Cimetière du Vieux-Château — the town’s highest terrace
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Climbed past the basilica to the cemetery at the top of the Vieux-Château, the highest terrace in town.

experiences.name: Sunrise over Garavan bay, Italy along the coast
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Watched the sun come up over Garavan bay toward Italy.

experiences.name: Back down the rampes by half past six
Breakfast on Rue Saint-Michel
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Down by half past six; breakfast, and the train back along the Riviera.

experiences.name: Train back along the Riviera

berlin-2026.occasion: The middle leg of a midsummer swing — Paris behind, Kraków ahead

days.title: An evening walk through the set pieces
days.summary: Arrived with hours of midsummer light left and went straight for the icons: the Brandenburg Gate at half past seven, dinner on the walk south, Potsdamer Platz, Checkpoint Charlie at nine, and back to the Reichstag lawn for the 9:30 sunset, finishing on the Spree bend as the light went.

experiences.name: Arrive and drop bags in Mitte
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Both walks started from the door — the Gate one way, the zoo the other.

experiences.name: The Brandenburg Gate in evening light
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: The Brandenburg Gate at 7:36 PM, still full of light.

experiences.name: Pariser Platz
Dinner on the walk south
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Dinner somewhere along the way.

experiences.name: Potsdamer Platz
Checkpoint Charlie at nine
Sunset on the Reichstag lawn
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Back across to the Reichstag just in time for the 9:30 sunset.

experiences.name: The Spree bend at the Chancellery
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Finishing on the Spree bend as the light went.

days.title: Morning with the animals
days.summary: A slow start and coffee in City West, then a late morning at the Zoologischer Garten on the Tiergarten’s west edge — out past the gates after noon, with the afternoon off-camera and the morning train to Kraków ahead.

experiences.name: Slow morning, coffee in City West
Zoologischer Garten — an unhurried loop
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: An unhurried hour among the enclosures, camera out in bursts.

krakow-2026.occasion: Third leg of the June swing — Berlin behind, the Riviera ahead

days.title: Into the Old Town
days.summary: A slow first morning after the long train day, then the medieval core mid-afternoon — the Rynek and Cloth Hall, St. Mary’s, Floriańska to the Gate and Barbican — and an evening down Grodzka to the Vistula boulevards below Wawel, back up Kanonicza at dusk.

experiences.name: A slow morning after the train day
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: A slow first morning after the long train day.

experiences.name: Rynek Główny — the Cloth Hall and St. Mary’s
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Out into the medieval core mid-afternoon: the Rynek and its Cloth Hall, St. Mary’s towers.

experiences.name: Up Floriańska to the Gate and the Barbican
experiences.placeName: St. Florian’s Gate and the Barbican

experiences.name: Mały Rynek on the loop back
Break at the flat before dinner
Dinner in the Old Town
Down Grodzka, past Sts. Peter & Paul
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: The evening ran the other way — down Grodzka past Sts. Peter & Paul.

experiences.name: The Vistula boulevards below Wawel
Back up Kanonicza at dusk

days.title: The Rynek, then Oświęcim
days.summary: A late morning back on the square and a long lunch in the Old Town, then the trip’s necessary counterweight: out to Oświęcim — Birkenau first, the ramp and barracks and the long walk to the memorial, then Auschwitz I in the early evening, and the quiet ride back.

experiences.name: Late morning on the Rynek
A long lunch, then a wander back through the Old Town
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: A late morning back on the square and a long lunch in the Old Town.

experiences.name: Out to Oświęcim
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Out to Oświęcim in the early afternoon.

experiences.name: Auschwitz II–Birkenau — the ramp, the barracks, the far memorial
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Birkenau first — the ramp, the barracks, the long walk to the memorial at the far end.

experiences.name: Auschwitz I in the early evening
The quiet ride back to Kraków
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Then Auschwitz I in the early evening, and the quiet ride back to Kraków.

days.title: Ten to five on the empty square
days.summary: Up before the sun for one last walk — the Rynek completely empty at ten to five, first light on St. Mary’s towers, half an hour with the square to myself, then coffee, the bags, and the flight south to the Riviera.

experiences.name: The Rynek, empty, at ten to five
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: The Rynek at 4:49 AM, completely empty.

experiences.name: First light on St. Mary’s towers
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: First light catching St. Mary’s towers.

experiences.name: One last loop of the square
Coffee, and the bags
The flight south to the Riviera
experiences.sourceNarrativeExcerpt: Then coffee, the bags, and the flight south to the Riviera.
